title: Committed generated plugin is stale and missing the DoR gate entirely (NFR-5 / AC8)
verification: git ls-files confirms .ados-claude/agents/readiness-reviewer.md, .ados-claude/skills/check-readiness/SKILL.md, and .ados-claude/agents/pm.md are now tracked. Committed .ados-claude/agents/pm.md line 275 reads '5. **dor_check** — Delegate to @readiness-reviewer; hard gate before delivery'. Remediation commit 3ce7f32 regenerated and committed the plugin alongside its .opencode/ sources.

title: CI freshness gate does not compare fresh build vs committed plugin (enables NFR-5 drift)
verification: New test case 'committed plugin matches fresh build' builds into a temp dir and diffs against committed .ados-claude/. Full suite now reports 16/16 passed, proving committed==fresh (NFR-5 enforced for source-vs-generated drift).

title: Plan execution log + all Phase A-G checkboxes never updated (DONE_BUT_UNCHECKED)
verification: rg '\- \[ \]' returns 0 unchecked boxes (UNCHECKED_EXIT=1). All 39 task checkboxes are '- [x]'. Execution log rows for Phases A-H filled with status=Done, dates, and commit SHAs (f7678bb, 5257098, bd8f7ac, 7291199, edf87fc, 3ce7f32).

title: Change's own pm-notes phase map omits dor_check (DM-1 / AC8)
verification: pm-notes.yaml line 17 now contains 'dor_check: { started: ..., completed: ... }' between delivery_planning and delivery, with an annotation explaining the RT1 red-team served as GH-57's own DoR surrogate.

title: Stale OQ-1 narrative contradicts its own later reversal (cross-artifact inconsistency)
verification: Plan lines 65-69 now state OQ-1 RESOLVED via RT1-MAJOR-03 as 'YES — one-line DoR cross-reference note', explicitly noting it 'reverses the earlier no-edits stance'. Out-of-scope bullet (lines 143-144) carries matching reversal note. Consistent with spec §14 / Phase B.4.

title: Working tree left dirty (uncommitted regenerated plugin) at review time
verification: git status --porcelain is empty at review time. The regeneration was committed in 3ce7f32.

title: Command frontmatter model (sonnet) differs from agent model (opus) — confirm intentional
verification: Confirmed intentional — matches the established /review (sonnet) + @reviewer (opus) pattern: lightweight command wrapper delegates heavy reasoning to the opus-tier agent. No change required.
